projects.name: Koan.Cache
projects.purpose: Pillar core (~4,000 LOC): static Cache facade, CacheClient (reader/writer + singleflight), LayeredCache L1/L2 orchestrator, CoherenceCoordinator (IHostedService routing channel invalidations to L1), policy registry/materializer/bootstrapper, [Cacheable] repository decorator, and the built-in Memory L1 store (folded in from the deleted Adapter.Memory package).
projects.state: active/settled — rich KoanAutoRegistrar boot report with per-policy health flags (OK/DEGRADED/BUG), exactly 1 TODO in the whole project (GetMany batch caching pass-through at Decorators/CachedRepository.cs:124). Carries Gen-1 residue: CacheAdapterResolver (165-line reflection scanner), [Obsolete] AddKoanCacheAdapter shim, MemoryAutoRegistrar/MemoryCacheAdapterRegistrar (reports defunct module name 'Koan.Cache.Adapter.Memory'), a leftover Koan.Cache.Adapter.Memory.csproj.lscache file, stale doc comment in LayeredCache ('M3 will wire that integration; M2 leaves coherence inactive' — coherence IS wired), and InternalsVisibleTo grants to two nonexistent test assemblies (Koan.Cache.Tests, Koan.Tests.Cache.Unit).

projects.name: Koan.Cache.Abstractions
projects.purpose: Kernel-tier contracts (~1,335 LOC): store/client/builder interfaces, primitives (keys, options, results), policy attributes, coherence channel contracts, and the ARCH-0081 typed registration helpers. Referenced by Koan.Data.Core for the EntityContext.CacheBehavior integration.
keyTypes: ICoherenceChannel<TMessage> (generic, explicitly designed for future promotion to Koan.Core.Coherence)
projects.state: active/settled — minimal deps (only MS config/DI abstractions), clean; one Gen-1 leftover folder (Adapters/: CacheAdapterDescriptor, ICacheAdapterRegistrar) whose only live consumer is the obsolete shim chain in Koan.Cache.

projects.name: Koan.Cache.Adapter.Memory
projects.purpose: Former standalone Memory L1 adapter package; its code now lives at src/Koan.Cache/Adapters/Memory.
projects.state: tombstone — directory is completely empty, no csproj, not in Koan.sln; mentioned only in docs/architecture/foundation-consolidation-plan.md and docs/assessment/README.md.

projects.name: Koan.Cache.Adapter.Redis
projects.purpose: Redis L2 store + Redis pub/sub coherence channel in one package; rides the shared IConnectionMultiplexer from Koan.Data.Connector.Redis (ARCH-0080 shared transport ownership).
keyTypes: RedisCacheStore : ICacheStore ([ProviderPriority(100)], Remote placement, full capability set: tags/sliding/SWR/binary/persistence)
projects.state: active — integration tests (RedisCacheAdapterSpec, RedisCoherenceCornerstoneSpec) go through KoanIntegrationHost per ARCH-0079 canon.

projects.name: Koan.Cache.Adapter.Sqlite
projects.purpose: Persistent on-disk L1 store (survives process restart) with tag indexing and TTL eviction; [ProviderPriority(50)] preempts the built-in Memory store (10) by mere reference.
projects.state: active — SqliteCachePersistenceSpec uses KoanIntegrationHost; also exercised by the CrossEngine behavior oracle.

projects.name: Koan.Cache.Analyzers
projects.purpose: Single Roslyn analyzer (KOAN0001, netstandard2.0): flags bare TryAddEnumerable(ServiceDescriptor.Singleton<I>(...)) on cache interfaces, which produces an indistinguishable descriptor that throws at boot; steers authors to the ARCH-0081 typed helpers. Wired as analyzer-only ref into all five cache runtime projects.
keyTypes: KnownInterfaces map (internal; documented as 'adding a new pillar interface to the canon: append here')
projects.state: active but NOT in Koan.sln (neither is its test project Koan.Tests.Cache.Analyzers) — builds only transitively via analyzer ProjectReferences; csproj carries hard-won NuGet packaging lessons from the 0.8.0 first release.

projects.name: Koan.Cache.Coherence.InMemory
projects.purpose: In-process coherence channel (~189 LOC): shared InMemoryCoherenceBus lets multiple channels in one process simulate cross-node delivery. Primary use: tests and single-node coherence-wiring verification. Priority int.MinValue so any real transport preempts.
projects.state: active — hosts the coherence cornerstone + startup-tolerance specs; no production consumers in src/ or samples/.

projects.name: Koan.Cache.Coherence.Messaging
projects.purpose: Coherence over Koan.Messaging (~227 LOC): rides an existing IMessageProxy bus instead of standing up Redis pub/sub; [ProviderPriority(150)] preempts RedisCoherenceChannel (100) when both are present.
projects.state: active — channel + envelope specs in tests/Suites/Cache/Coherence.Messaging.

cohesion: Highest-cohesion pillar surveyed — this IS the reference for 'settled' Koan architecture. Clean three-band layering: Abstractions (Kernel, zero Koan deps) ← Koan.Cache (core + default Memory L1) ← adapters/channels that activate by reference via KoanAutoRegistrar + ARCH-0081 typed helpers + ProviderPriority preemption (Memory 10 < Sqlite 50 L1; Redis-pubsub 100 < Messaging 150 coherence). Storage is strictly separated from coherence (the structural fix ARCH-0075 made over the Gen-1 ARCH-0059 scaffolding, which conflated them), LayeredCache is composition-not-inheritance with a closed verb set, and the architectural invariant (remote invalidation touches L1 only) is enforced in one place and documented at every call site. Seams/fractures: (1) the Gen-1 adapter-registrar machinery (CacheAdapterResolver reflection scanner, ICacheAdapterRegistrar, CacheAdapterDescriptor, [Obsolete] AddKoanCacheAdapter, MemoryAutoRegistrar reporting a module name for a package that no longer exists) still lives inside the otherwise Gen-2 pillar; (2) every cache project depends on Koan.Data.Abstractions purely for ProviderPriorityAttribute — a cross-pillar gravity leak; (3) ICoherenceChannel<T> is explicitly written as 'promote to Koan.Core.Coherence once a second consumer materializes' — and the fleet-trust CAEP-epoch-revocation direction is exactly that consumer; (4) sln hygiene: Koan.Cache.Analyzers and two of nine test projects (Analyzers, CrossEngine) are not in Koan.sln; (5) the web-facing piece (KoanCacheControlMiddleware) lives in Koan.Web but its tests live under tests/Suites/Cache/Web — fine, but the pillar boundary crosses there.
redundancies: Dual adapter-registration models coexist: legacy ICacheAdapterRegistrar/CacheAdapterResolver/AddKoanCacheAdapter (obsolete, reflection-scanning) vs current AddCacheStore<T>/AddCoherenceChannel<T> typed helpers + per-package KoanAutoRegistrar. Only the latter is used by real adapters; the former survives solely to serve itself.

Two IKoanAutoRegistrar implementations inside the single Koan.Cache assembly (KoanAutoRegistrar + MemoryAutoRegistrar); the Memory one exists only to emit a boot-report module named 'Koan.Cache.Adapter.Memory' — a package that no longer exists — and to call the obsolete shim.

ADR overlap: ARCH-0059 is formally superseded by ARCH-0075, but ARCH-0060 (control surface: Exists/CacheTagSet/flush helpers) is still status=approved with no cross-reference reconciling it against the 0075 rebuild — readers must diff mentally.

ICoherenceChannel<TMessage> + ICacheCoherenceChannel marker pair is intentional (DI-resolvable specialization) and documented, not accidental — but it duplicates the channel concept until the planned Koan.Core promotion happens.
CacheKeyTemplate (283 lines, general-purpose token templating) sits in the Decorators namespace despite serving policies and the EntityCache helpers too.
csproj Description for Koan.Cache still advertises 'singleflight coordination' although Singleflight was lifted to Koan.Core.Singleflight (the project now only consumes it).
deadOrExperimental: src/Koan.Cache.Adapter.Memory — empty tombstone directory (no csproj, not in sln); code folded into src/Koan.Cache/Adapters/Memory
src/Koan.Cache/Adapters/Memory/Koan.Cache.Adapter.Memory.csproj.lscache — leftover tooling-cache file from the fold-in
tests/Suites/Cache/Unit/Koan.Tests.Cache.Unit — tombstone test dir containing only TestResults/ (no csproj, no specs), yet still named in Koan.Cache's InternalsVisibleTo

Gen-1 legacy chain: src/Koan.Cache/Adapters/CacheAdapterResolver.cs (165-line assembly scanner), src/Koan.Cache.Abstractions/Adapters/{CacheAdapterDescriptor,ICacheAdapterRegistrar}.cs, AddKoanCacheAdapter [Obsolete] shim, MemoryAutoRegistrar.cs, MemoryCacheAdapterRegistrar.cs — no consumer outside the chain itself
InternalsVisibleTo('Koan.Cache.Tests') and InternalsVisibleTo('Koan.Tests.Cache.Unit') in src/Koan.Cache/InternalsVisibleTo.cs — both target assemblies do not exist
consolidationCandidates: Cut the entire Gen-1 adapter-registrar chain (CacheAdapterResolver, ICacheAdapterRegistrar, CacheAdapterDescriptor, AddKoanCacheAdapter, MemoryAutoRegistrar, MemoryCacheAdapterRegistrar; ~350 LOC + a reflection/AssemblyLoadContext scanner) because ARCH-0075's own premise was 'no public consumers — break-and-rebuild authorized'; keeping an [Obsolete] back-compat shim with zero constituency contradicts the redesign discipline, and MemoryAutoRegistrar actively misreports a defunct module in boot provenance.
Delete tombstones: src/Koan.Cache.Adapter.Memory dir, tests/Suites/Cache/Unit dir, the .csproj.lscache leftover, and the two dead InternalsVisibleTo grants — pure hygiene, zero risk.

Merge Koan.Cache.Coherence.InMemory into Koan.Cache (189 LOC) — same rationale that folded the Memory store in: it is the priority-int.MinValue fallback used by tests/single-process verification, so 'Reference = Intent' buys nothing a CacheOptions flag couldn't; would cut one project. Counter-consideration: keeping it separate keeps any channel registration opt-in, so this is a judgment call the Memory-store precedent already answered.

Promote ICoherenceChannel<TMessage> (+CoherenceCapabilities, cursor/catch-up semantics, and arguably CoherenceCoordinator's channel-selection logic) to Koan.Core.Coherence — the file itself documents this as the plan once a second consumer exists, and the fleet-trust-fabric direction (CAEP-epoch-over-coherence-channel revocation) is that consumer. A file move with no renames, per its own remarks.

Generalize Koan.Cache.Analyzers into a framework-wide Koan.Analyzers: the KOAN0001 KnownInterfaces map is explicitly designed for 'adding a new pillar interface to the canon', i.e. the indistinguishable-descriptor bug class is not cache-specific; one analyzer package for ~113 projects beats one per pillar.

Move ProviderPriorityAttribute from Koan.Data.Abstractions to Koan.Core — both coherence packages (and CoherenceCoordinator's channel ordering) take a Data.Abstractions dependency for this single attribute; relocating it removes a cross-pillar edge from at least 3 cache projects and matches its actual cross-pillar usage (data adapters, cache stores, coherence channels, repository decorators).

Sln hygiene: add src/Koan.Cache.Analyzers, tests/.../Koan.Tests.Cache.Analyzers, and tests/.../Koan.Tests.Cache.CrossEngine to Koan.sln so solution-level test discovery and IDE loading see them (they currently build/run only when targeted directly or transitively).

ADR hygiene: annotate ARCH-0060 with its post-ARCH-0075 status (the control surface it defines — Exists, CacheTagSet, tag flush — survived the rebuild, so 'reaffirmed by 0075' is probably the right marker), and fix the stale M2/M3 milestone comment in LayeredCache plus the 'singleflight coordination' csproj description.

maturitySignals.tests: Strongest test posture of a periphery pillar: 9 dedicated suites under tests/Suites/Cache (~28 spec files): Abstractions (9 unit specs: attributes, invalidation incl. corner cases, key building, TTL policy, write options, capabilities), Topology (8: LayeredCache, topology resolver, policy materializer, decorator priority, EntityContext.CacheBehavior, health check, instrumentation, trace filter), Coherence.InMemory (cornerstone + startup-tolerance with a FailingCoherenceChannel fake), Coherence.Messaging (channel + envelope), Adapter.Redis (adapter + coherence cornerstone via RedisCacheNode/KoanIntegrationHost — real AddKoan() discovery per ARCH-0079 canon), Adapter.Sqlite (persistence via KoanIntegrationHost), CrossEngine (shared behavior-oracle base run against Memory and Sqlite engines — the FilterConvergence pattern applied to cache), Analyzers (KOAN0001 spec reflecting on KnownInterfaces), Web (middleware unit + TestServer). Gaps: Analyzers and CrossEngine test projects absent from Koan.sln; the Unit suite is a tombstone; CrossEngine oracle does not include a Redis engine (Redis covered separately but not against the shared spec base); GetMany batch caching is untested because unimplemented.
maturitySignals.docs: Excellent for this repo: 4 cache ADRs with explicit supersession lineage (ARCH-0059 superseded-by-0075, ARCH-0060 control surface, ARCH-0075 pillar rebuild, ARCH-0078 fresh-or-null/SWR-opt-in) plus two adjacent (ARCH-0080 shared transport, ARCH-0081 typed helpers + analyzer — the analyzer csproj even links the ADR in its helpLinkUri); docs/reference/data/cache.md (v0.7.0, TL;DR + boot-report walkthrough); docs/architecture/koan-cache-module.md; a dedicated .claude/skills/koan-caching skill; a full CLAUDE.md section. No per-project README.md in any src/Koan.Cache* directory, and docs/guides has no caching guide (reference doc fills that role). ARCH-0060's status relative to 0075 is the one documented-decision gap.
maturitySignals.codeQuality: Exemplary: exactly 1 TODO across ~7,200 source LOC (CachedRepository GetMany batch strategy); zero HACK/FIXME/WIP/NotImplementedException; NotSupportedException appears only as legitimate capability guards; XML doc comments cite ADR numbers at decision points (ARCH-0076 priority bands, ARCH-0078 SWR surfacing, ARCH-0079 degraded-mode rationale); invariants enforced and self-checked (boot report computes per-policy OK/DEGRADED/BUG health, materializer rejects L1>L2 TTL at boot); nullable enabled everywhere; consistent naming. Blemishes are all residue, not rot: the Gen-1 legacy chain, one stale milestone comment in LayeredCache, a stale csproj description, dead InternalsVisibleTo grants, and the .lscache leftover.
